title: Identify Layers in a Layered Architecture

description.task: Write a Java program that simulates a simple 3-layer architecture (Presentation, Business Logic, Data Access). Create three separate classes representing each layer and demonstrate a request flowing through them.
instructions: Create a DataRepository class with a method findUserById(int id) that returns a hardcoded user name string.
Create a BusinessService class that uses DataRepository to get user data and returns a formatted message.
Create a UserController class that uses BusinessService and prints the result.
In Main, demonstrate a request: UserController -> BusinessService -> DataRepository.
description.hint: Each layer should only communicate with the layer directly below or above it. This enforces separation of concerns.

description.task: Implement a simple MVC (Model-View-Controller) pattern in Java for a Student management scenario. The Model holds data, the View displays it, and the Controller mediates.
instructions: Complete the Student model with id, name, and grade fields plus getters and setters.
Implement StudentView with a printStudentDetails(Student s) method.
Implement StudentController with methods to get/set student name and grade, and to update the view.
In Main, create a student, display them, update a field, and display again.
description.hint: The Controller never directly manipulates the View fields; it calls View methods. The View never modifies the Model directly.
description.expectedOutput:
Student: Alice | Grade: 90.0
Updated Student: Alice | Grade: 95.0

description.task: Refactor a poorly written monolithic Java class into separate classes that follow the Separation of Concerns principle. The original class mixes UI, business logic, and data access.
description.context: Separation of Concerns (SoC) is a design principle where each module/class has responsibility over a single part of the program's functionality.
instructions: Create OrderRepository with a method getOrderAmount(int id) returning a double.
Create OrderService with a method calculateTotal(double amount) that adds 10% tax.
Create OrderView with a method displayReceipt(int orderId, double total) that prints the receipt.
Create a refactored OrderProcessor class that coordinates the three classes.
Output must match the expected output exactly.
description.hint: Each class should do ONE thing: repository fetches, service computes, view displays.
description.expectedOutput:
Fetching order 42 from database
Order total with tax: 110.0
Displaying receipt for order 42
